quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 11, Grade 10 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Cell Biology: Advanced Topics & Specialized Cells
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Membrane Structure, Cell Types, Organelle Functions, Plant vs. Animal Cell Differences

questions.question: Plasma membrane in eukaryotic cells is primarily composed of:
options: Lipids only forming a simple barrier
questions.explanation: The plasma membrane follows the fluid mosaic model, consisting of a phospholipid bilayer with embedded and peripheral proteins. Carbohydrates may be attached (glycoproteins/glycolipids) but are not primary components.

questions.question: Which is the oldest known type of organism on Earth?
questions.explanation: Bacteria are prokaryotes that appeared approximately 3.5 billion years ago. They are the oldest known life forms on Earth, with fossil evidence in stromatolites.

questions.question: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum looks rough under the microscope because of the attachment of which organelle to its surface?
questions.explanation: Rough ER has ribosomes attached to its cytoplasmic surface, giving it a 'rough' appearance. These ribosomes synthesize proteins that enter the ER for processing.

questions.question: Which cell part is not present in a plant cell but is found in an animal cell?
questions.explanation: Centrosomes (containing centrioles) are present in animal cells and help organize microtubules during cell division. Plant cells lack centrosomes.

questions.question: Which cell organelle is known as the protein factory of the cell?
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are called protein factories because they are the sites of protein synthesis (translation).

questions.question: Where is mitochondria located?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are organelles suspended in the cytoplasm, where they produce ATP through cellular respiration. They are not attached to other structures but can move within the cell.

questions.question: Which part of the plant cell is the chloroplast?
options: The jelly-like material surrounding the nucleus
The rigid outer covering of the cell
The round structure that stores genetic material
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts are green, oval-shaped organelles containing chlorophyll. They conduct photosynthesis. Cytoplasm is jelly-like, cell wall is rigid, and nucleus stores genetic material.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for packaging and transporting proteins in the cell?
questions.explanation: The Golgi apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages proteins into vesicles for transport to their destinations. Ribosomes synthesize proteins, ER processes them, and lysosomes digest waste.

questions.question: Which organelle is responsible for breaking down waste materials in animal cells?
questions.explanation: Lysosomes contain hydrolytic enzymes that break down waste materials, old organelles, and pathogens. They are the digestive system of the cell.

questions.question: The smallest cells present in the human body (by volume measure) are:
questions.explanation: Sperm cells are the smallest human cells by volume. They are highly specialized for motility, with most of the cell volume consisting of nucleus and flagellum.

questions.question: The smallest cells present in the human body (by length measure) are:
questions.explanation: Red blood cells are approximately 7-8 μm in diameter, making them the smallest by length. Sperm cells are longer due to their tail but smaller by volume. Nerve cells are the longest cells in the body.

questions.question: The smallest cells known to man are:
questions.explanation: Bacteria are typically 0.2-2.0 μm in diameter, making them among the smallest cells. Some bacteria like Mycoplasma are even smaller (0.1-0.3 μm). Amoeba are large eukaryotic cells.

questions.question: Plant cells in which chloroplast isn't present are known as:
questions.explanation: Epidermal cells in plants typically lack chloroplasts, allowing light to penetrate to photosynthetic tissues below. Guard cells (surrounding stomata) do contain chloroplasts for stomatal function.

questions.question: Which plant cells typically lack chloroplasts?
options: Root hair cells and xylem parenchyma
questions.explanation: Root hair cells and xylem parenchyma are underground or non-photosynthetic tissues and lack chloroplasts. Mesophyll cells (palisade and spongy) are photosynthetic and contain many chloroplasts. Guard cells contain chloroplasts for stomatal function.

questions.question: What part of a plant plays an important role in reproduction?
questions.explanation: Flowers are the reproductive structures in angiosperms, containing male (stamens) and female (carpels) reproductive organs. Leaves perform photosynthesis, stems provide support, and roots absorb water/nutrients.

questions.question: Plant and animal cells are unable to reproduce without the presence of:
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains genetic material (DNA) that directs cell division. Without the nucleus, cells cannot replicate their DNA or undergo mitosis/meiosis. Prokaryotes reproduce without a nucleus, but eukaryotic cells require it.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered advanced cell biology concepts.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review specialized cell types and organelle functions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on plant vs. animal cell differences and cell sizes.
scoreMessages.0: Please review cell biology fundamentals and specialized cell types.
